REACHABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_BUILD: Fire enabled transitions in the Petri net to explore the reachability graph step by step.
REACHABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_MOVE: Left-click and drag state nodes to customize the layout.
REACHABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_RESET: Double-click a state node to reset the Petri net to this marking. Afterwards, firing can be continued from this Petri Net state.
REACHABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_VALIDATE: Validate the Reachability Graph and check for completeness. This is based on the current state of the Petri Net and checks whether all transitions that can be fired from the current state have been explored. Does not function while the automatically generated full RG is shown.
REACHABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_TOGGLEVIEW: Switches between the view shown in the course script and a view where markings are displayed at each StateNode of the RG.
REACHABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_SHOWCOMPLETEGRAPH: With this, the complete Reachability Graph is shown in case of a limited RG. For an unlimited RG, the partial RG up to a critical Marking m' is shown.
REACHABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_HIDECOMPLETEGRAPH: Hides the auto-generated complete Reachability Graph and switches view back to the User-generated Graph.

COVERABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_BUILD: Fire enabled transitions in the Petri net to explore the coverability graph step by step.
COVERABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_MOVE: Left-click and drag state nodes to customize the layout.
COVERABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_RESET: Double-click a state node to reset the Petri net to this marking. Afterwards, firing can be continued from this Petri Net state.
COVERABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_VALIDATE: Validate the Coverability Graph and check for completeness. This is based on the current state of the Petri Net and checks whether all transitions that can be fired from the current state have been explored. Does not function while the automatically generated full CG is shown.
COVERABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_TOGGLEVIEW: Switches between the view shown in the course script and a view where markings are displayed at each StateNode of the CG.
COVERABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_SHOWCOMPLETEGRAPH: With this, the complete Coverability Graph is shown.
COVERABILITY_GRAPH.INSTRUCTION_HIDECOMPLETEGRAPH: Hides the auto-generated complete Coverability Graph and switches view back to the User-generated Graph.

CONFIRM_DIALOG.TITLE: Switch Mode
CONFIRM_DIALOG.MESSAGE_DRAW: Do you want to keep the current drawing?
CONFIRM_DIALOG.MESSAGE_DEFAULT: Do you want to keep the current content?

RGMARKING_DIALOG.TITLE: Input Marking
RGMARKING_DIALOG.MESSAGE_DEFAULT: Please input the correct marking of the Petri Net after firing.

CGMARKING_DIALOG.TITLE: Input Marking
CGMARKING_DIALOG.MESSAGE_DEFAULT: Please input the correct marking of the Petri Net after firing. For Omega please input small letter w.
CGMARKING_DIALOG.DESCRIPTION: For Omega please input small letter w.

MODE_INFO_TOOLTIP: Use the slider to switch between two modes: The Learning Mode helps you better understand the various areas. The Exam Mode is for your exam preparation with less support from the application and fewer hints.

TUPLE_INPUT.DESCRIPTION: Enter a Petri net tuple in the format:
TUPLE_INPUT.LABEL: Tuple String
TUPLE_INPUT.TOAST_ERROR_BODY: Could not parse the provided tuple.
TUPLE_INPUT.TOAST_INVALIDATION_BODY: Tuple and drawing do not match

VALIDATION.ARC_WEIGHT_MISMATCH: Arc weight mismatch at {{arc}}: expected {{expected}}, found {{found}}

DRAW.TOAST_DUPLICATE_LABEL_BODY: The label '{{label}}' is already used. Please choose a unique label.

INSTRUCTION.DRAG_DROP: Drag elements from the palette to the canvas to create your Petri net.
INSTRUCTION.LEFT_CLICK_MOVE: Left-click and drag to move elements around the canvas and from the palette to the canvas.
INSTRUCTION.RIGHT_CLICK_CONNECT: Right-click a place or transition, then right-click the opposite type to create a connection.
INSTRUCTION.MIDDLE_CLICK_DELETE: Middle-click an element or connection to delete it.
INSTRUCTION.DOUBLE_CLICK_EDIT_LABEL: Double-click an element to edit its label.
INSTRUCTION.SCROLL_CHANGE_TOKENS_WEIGHT: Use the mouse wheel to change the number of tokens (places) or the weight (connection).

TOGGLE.SHOW_DRAWING_TOOLTIP: Toggle to show or hide the drawing on the canvas. The tuple specification is always visible.

HEADER.RG_INFO: Switch back to the self-created Graph necessary
HEADER.CG_INFO: Switch back to the self-created Graph necessary

BODY.NO_NET_TO_SAVE: There is no Petri net to save.
BODY.TRANSITION_NOT_PRESENT: The transition {{label}} is not present in the petri net.
BODY.TRANSITION_NOT_ACTIVATED: The transition {{label}} is not activated and cannot be fired.
BODY.MODE_SWITCHED_EXAM: You are now in exam mode.
BODY.MODE_SWITCHED_LEARN: You are now in learning mode.
BODY.FILE_EMPTY_OR_UNREADABLE: The selected file is empty or could not be read.
BODY.DOWNLOAD_FAILED: File at {{url}} could not be loaded.{{details}}
BODY.EMPTY_RESPONSE: No content was returned from the server at {{url}}.
BODY.NET_LOADED_SUCCESSFULLY_HIDDEN: Petri net was successfully loaded, but some elements were hidden due to exam mode restrictions.
BODY.FILE_NOT_INTERPRETABLE: The file could not be interpreted.
BODY.CRITICAL_PARSING_ERROR: A critical error occurred while parsing the file.
BODY.LOAD_NET_FIRST: Please load a Petri net first.
BODY.VALIDATION_ERROR: Please load a Petri net first.
BODY.VALIDATION_VALID_MAXIMAL: The process net is valid and maximal.
BODY.INVALID_SEQUENCES: The following firing sequences are invalid. Hover over the info icon next to each invalid sequence in the table to see details.
BODY.SEQUENCE_GENERATION: Generated firing sequences of the Petri net with minimum {{minTransitionCount}} transitions per sequence and a total of {{maxSequenceCount}} sequences. This represents a bounded sublanguage of the Petri net.
BODY.SWITCHED_STATE_SUCCESSFULLY: Petri Net marking was successfully switched to corresponding state.
BODY.STATENODE_EXISTING: StateNode already exists, new arc created, state of RG was updated.
BODY.STATENODE_ARC_EXISTING: StateNode and arc already exist, state of RG was updated.
BODY.MARKING_INPUT_WRONG: The input marking does not match the target marking.
BODY.MARKING_DIALOG_DISMISSED: No entry made, node created automatically.
BODY.RG_CHECK_SUCCESS: The created reachability graph is complete and correct.
BODY.SWITCH_BACK_TO_USER_GRAPH: Please switch back to your self-created Graph to continue editing.
BODY.CG_CHECK_SUCCESS: The created coverability graph is complete and correct.
BODY.CG_SWITCH_BACK_TO_USER_GRAPH: Please switch back to your self-created Graph to continue editing.

VALIDATION_MESSAGES.TRANSITION_NO_MATCH: ❌ Transition {{label}} has no matching label in the Petri net.
VALIDATION_MESSAGES.PRESET_WEIGHT_MISMATCH: ❌ Preset weight mismatch at {{label}} for place {{place}}. Expected: {{expected}} / Found: {{found}}
VALIDATION_MESSAGES.POSTSET_WEIGHT_MISMATCH: ❌ Postset weight mismatch at {{label}} for place {{place}}. Expected: {{expected}} / Found: {{found}}
VALIDATION_MESSAGES.PLACE_NO_INCOMING: ❌ Place {{label}} has no incoming arc, although it is not a start place in this process net.
VALIDATION_MESSAGES.PLACE_MULTIPLE_PRODUCERS: ❌ Place {{place}} has more than one producer – violates causality.
VALIDATION_MESSAGES.PLACE_MULTIPLE_CONSUMERS: ❌ Place {{place}} has more than one consumer – violates no-conflict property.
VALIDATION_MESSAGES.CYCLE_DETECTED: ❌ Process net contains a cycle – process nets must be acyclic.
VALIDATION_MESSAGES.MISSING_START_PLACES: ❌ Start places missing in process net: {{places}}. Please place all marked places.
VALIDATION_MESSAGES.MARKED_START_PLACES_NOT_IN_NET: ❌ The marked start places ({{places}}) are not in the process net.
VALIDATION_MESSAGES.NOT_MAXIMAL: Info: Process net is not maximal. Transition {{label}} can still be fired with places ({{places}}).

PROCESS_NET.HELP_TOOLTIP: Click to open the usage guide
PROCESS_NET.INSTRUCTION_DRAG_DROP: Drag elements from the left display area here to create your process net.
PROCESS_NET.INSTRUCTION_LEFT_CLICK_MOVE: Left-click and drag to move elements around the canvas.
PROCESS_NET.INSTRUCTION_RIGHT_CLICK_CONNECT: Right-click a place or transition, then right-click the opposite type to create a connection.
PROCESS_NET.INSTRUCTION_MIDDLE_CLICK_DELETE: Middle-click an element to delete it.
PROCESS_NET.INSTRUCTION_MIDDLE_CLICK_DELETE_CONN: Middle-click a connection to delete it.
PROCESS_NET.INSTRUCTION_VALIDATE_TOAST: Click the checklist button to validate the process net.
PROCESS_NET.INSTRUCTION_AUTO_FIRING_TEXT: In learn mode, clicking activated transitions in the Petri net automatically builds the process net.
